01_initial_request.target_name: Amino acid antiporters (including ASCT1, ASCT2, LAT1, LAT2, y+LAT1, y+LAT2, asc-1, xCT)
01_initial_request.input_data: Xenopus laevis oocytes expressing amino acid antiporters and 12C/13C-labelled amino acid mixtures
01_initial_request.user_intent: To systematically investigate whether substrate specificity of amino acid antiporters differs between inward and outward facing conformations (asymmetric antiport)
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Quantification of bidirectional amino acid transport fluxes to determine substrate specificity symmetry

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] mRNA for various transporters needs to be isolated from specific cell lines or tissues. [Gap] Pure mRNA is required for cDNA synthesis. [Decision] To achieve mRNA isolation, chose to use RNeasy Mini kit to perform RNA extraction on human brain, 143B, and A549 cells, expecting to obtain total RNA.

parameters.input_material: human brain tissue, 143B cells, A549 lung adenocarcinoma cells

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Total RNA has been extracted. [Gap] cDNA is needed for PCR amplification. [Decision] To achieve cDNA synthesis, chose to use SuperScript II reverse transcriptase to perform reverse transcription on 2 micrograms of total RNA, expecting to obtain single stranded DNA.

[Background] cDNA is available. [Gap] Coding DNA needs to be amplified with overlapping regions for cloning. [Decision] To achieve DNA amplification, chose to use Pfu or Taq DNA polymerases to perform PCR with primers designed for Gibson cloning, expecting to obtain PCR products.

parameters.primer_design: overlapping regions homologous to pGHJ cut at EcoRI site
parameters.extraction_kit: Monarch DNA gel extraction kit (New England BioLabs; T1020S)

references.relevance: Describes the pGHJ oocyte vector used for cloning.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PCR inserts and linearized vector are prepared. [Gap] Inserts need to be ligated into the vector. [Decision] To achieve cloning, chose to use Gibson assembly cloning kit to perform ligation of insert into pGHJ vector, expecting to obtain plasmids.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Clones with plasmids of expected size propagated.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Bacterial clones are grown. [Gap] Plasmid DNA needs to be purified for transcription. [Decision] To achieve plasmid purification, chose to use Wizard Plus SV Miniprep DNA purification system to isolate plasmids, expecting to obtain pure plasmid DNA.

tool.name: Wizard Plus SV Miniprep DNA purification system

parameters.verification: Sanger sequencing at Biomolecular Resource Facility (ANU)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Verified plasmids are available. [Gap] cRNA is needed for oocyte injection. [Decision] To achieve cRNA synthesis, chose to use mMessage mMachine T7 transcription kit to perform in vitro transcription on linearized plasmids, expecting to obtain capped RNA.

parameters.purification: phenol chloroform method and ethanol precipitation

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Capped RNA (cRNA) containing coding sequence of human amino acid exchanger produced.

references.citation: Broer, A. et al., 2001 [25]; Broer, A. et al., 2000 [26]
references.relevance: Previous cloning descriptions for LAT1, LAT2, y+LAT2.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] cRNA is ready. [Gap] Oocytes need to be prepared for injection. [Decision] To achieve oocyte defolliculation, chose to use Collagenase B to treat ovaries, expecting to obtain Stage V oocytes.

parameters.buffer: OR2- (82.3 mM NaCl, 2.5 mM KCl, 1 mM MgCl2, 1 mM NaHPO4, 5 mM HEPES; pH 7.8)

references.relevance: Ethical approval for frog surgery and oocyte preparation.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Stage V oocytes are selected. [Gap] Transporter mRNA needs to be introduced into oocytes. [Decision] To achieve expression, chose to use Microinjector to perform microinjection of cRNA into oocytes, expecting to obtain cRNA-expressing oocytes.

parameters.cRNA_amount: 5-15 ng depending on transporter (e.g., 10 ng hASCT2, 5 ng hLAT1)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Oocytes are injected. [Gap] Transporters need time to express on the membrane. [Decision] To achieve protein expression, chose to use Oocyte Incubation to maintain oocytes in buffer, expecting to obtain functional transporters on the membrane.

parameters.duration: five days (three days for LAT1 and LAT2)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Oocytes express transporters. [Gap] Intracellular amino acid pool needs to be elevated for exchange experiments. [Decision] To achieve preloading, chose to use Amino Acid Preloading Incubation to incubate oocytes in 12C amino acid mix, expecting to obtain elevated intracellular neutral and cationic amino acids.

parameters.concentration: 1 mM 12C amino acids (except glutamate and aspartate)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Elevated intracellular pool of neutral and cationic amino acids generated.

references.citation: Taylor, M.A. & Smith, L.D., 1987 [31]; Meier, C. et al., 2002 [16]
references.relevance: Reports on oocyte amino acid pools.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Oocytes are preloaded with 12C amino acids. [Gap] Bidirectional flux needs to be measured using labelled substrates. [Decision] To achieve flux measurement, chose to use Stable Isotope Flux Incubation to incubate oocytes in 13C amino acid mix, expecting to obtain trans-stimulation of transport activity.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Trans-stimulation of transport activity allowed to proceed.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Flux incubation is complete. [Gap] Metabolites need to be extracted from oocytes and supernatant. [Decision] To achieve metabolite isolation, chose to use Methanol/Chloroform Extraction to homogenize oocytes, expecting to obtain aqueous fraction containing amino acids.

[Background] Aqueous extracts are dried. [Gap] Amino acids need derivatization for LC-MS detection (except cysteine). [Decision] To achieve derivatization, chose to use Butylation Reagents to react with amino acids, expecting to obtain butylated amino acids.

[Background] Samples are derivatized. [Gap] Amino acids need quantification. [Decision] To achieve quantification, chose to use UltiMate 3000 UHPLC + Q-Exactive Plus to perform LC-MS analysis on C18 column, expecting to obtain amino acid concentrations.

parameters.column: Kinetex 1.7 µm C18 100 Å 100 x 2.1 mm

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Quantification of cystine and all proteinogenic amino acids except cysteine.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cysteine was not detected well with butylation. [Gap] Cysteine needs specific derivatization. [Decision] To achieve cysteine detection, chose to use N-ethylmaleimide (NEM) to react with sulfhydryl group, expecting to obtain derivatized cysteine.

references.relevance: Adapted method for thiol redox metabolome.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cysteine is derivatized. [Gap] Cysteine needs quantification. [Decision] To achieve quantification, chose to use UltiMate 3000 UHPLC + Q-Exactive Plus to perform LC-MS analysis on HILIC column, expecting to obtain cysteine concentrations.

parameters.column: SeQuant ZIC cHILIC 3 µm 100 Å 150 x 2.1 mm

references.relevance: Method for cysteine detection after blocking sulfhydryl group.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] LC-MS data suggests asymmetric flux for some substrates. [Gap] Specific asymmetric fluxes need validation with radiolabels. [Decision] To achieve validation, chose to use Scintillation Counter to perform radiolabelled flux experiments with 14C-substrates, expecting to obtain transport rates.

tool.name: Scintillation Counter
tool.version: PerkinElmer TriCarb 4910 TR or Hidex 300 SL

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Confirmed asymmetric flux for Gln (LAT2), Pro/Gly/Asp (ASCT1).

references.relevance: Previous report of asymmetric antiport for ASCT2.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] LC-MS raw data is acquired. [Gap] Data needs processing for quantification. [Decision] To achieve data processing, chose to use Xcalibur software suite to integrate area under the curve, expecting to obtain analyte ratios.

parameters.method: integrating the area under the curve (AUC)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] AUC ratios are available. [Gap] Concentrations need to be calculated using standards. [Decision] To achieve quantification, chose to use Skyline to process data against calibration curves, expecting to obtain transport rates.

parameters.formula: Rates calculated using oocyte number and volumes

references.relevance: Skyline ecosystem for quantitative mass spectrometry.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: LC-MS stable isotope tracing and Radiolabelled flux assays

net_import_export_rates.interpretation: Quantified bidirectional fluxes for 20 amino acids across 8 antiporters

symmetry_assessment.interpretation: Most antiporters equalize substrate pools; exceptions found for y+LAT1/2 (Na+ coupled) and ASCT1/2 (Gly/Pro/Asp)

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The initial objective was achieved. Antiporters were found to be largely symmetric in substrate specificity, with specific exceptions (y+LAT1, y+LAT2, ASCT1, ASCT2) displaying asymmetric transport modes often linked to ion coupling or allosteric effects.
